title: Cross-session modify: customer returns in new session to reschedule
failing_behavior: Customer booked Gel Manicure with Tracy in a previous session. Returns in new browser session, says 'I need to reschedule'. State is empty: services=[], staff=null, name=null, phone=null. Old prompt said 'carry over services from STATE' — but state is empty so nothing carried. Claude asks for date/time without knowing what services to reschedule. Final booking ends up with services=[] — empty appointment. Confirmation packet shows no service name.
root_cause: The modify flow assumed services/staff/name/phone were always in sessionStorage from the same session. Cross-session case: sessionStorage is gone. Prompt didn't instruct Claude to ask for phone first. No JS code existed to look up the existing booking by phone and pre-populate state.
fix_summary: Three changes: (1) Updated prompt MODIFY section: SAME-SESSION path (carry from state) vs CROSS-SESSION path (ask for phone first). (2) Added _xsBookingLookup() function: queries Firestore by phone, finds most recent active booking, pre-populates biz._bookingState.services/staff/name/existingBookingId. (3) Added trigger in send() else block: fires when pendingAction=modify_booking AND phone newly populated AND services=[]. Result: state is pre-populated, Claude's NEXT turn sees services/staff and asks only for new date/time.

conversation.content: I need to reschedule my appointment

Of course! Could you give me your phone number so I can look up your appointment?
[STATE:{"intent":"booking_request","services":[],"staff":null,"date":null,"time":null,"name":null,"phone":null,"lang":"en","pendingAction":"modify_booking","existingBookingId":null}]

Got it! Let me look that up.
[STATE:{"intent":"booking_request","services":[],"staff":null,"date":null,"time":null,"name":null,"phone":"4085551234","lang":"en","pendingAction":"modify_booking","existingBookingId":null}]
Found your appointment: Gel Manicure with Tracy on 2026-04-15 at 14:00. What new date and time would you like?

Let me check if Tracy is free on Thursday the 23rd at 11 AM...
[BOOKING:{"services":["Gel Manicure"],"staff":"Tracy","date":"2026-04-23","time":"11:00","name":"Jane Smith","phone":"4085551234","lang":"en"}]
[ESCALATE:appointment]
[STATE:{"intent":"booking_request","services":["Gel Manicure"],"staff":"Tracy","date":"2026-04-23","time":"11:00","name":"Jane Smith","phone":"4085551234","lang":"en","pendingAction":"modify_booking","existingBookingId":null}]

expected_firestore_action: update existing booking doc or create new + mark old as rescheduled
verify_fix_string_2: CROSS-SESSION: If services/name/phone are NOT in CURRENT BOOKING STATE
notes: The 'system_inject' in the conversation above represents the _xsBookingLookup() output injected into the chat by JS (not Claude). After injection, biz._bookingState has services/staff/name pre-populated, so Claude's next response sees them in the CURRENT BOOKING STATE context and proceeds correctly.
confidence_gap: Runner verifies: (1) _xsBookingLookup function exists in source, (2) CROSS-SESSION prompt instruction present. Live testing required to confirm: (3) Firestore lookup fires at correct moment, (4) pre-populated state is used in final booking, (5) modify flow produces correct booking with correct services.
